reasoning: The 10s2s Treasury yield spread has steepened sharply in recent weeks, jumping from 0.59 on Dec 11 to 0.68 on Dec 19. Such rapid expansions often overshoot local equilibria, leading to near-term mean reversion. The FRED statistical analysis estimates a 41% chance of exceeding 0.68 within the next week. As a result, the short-term probability sits below 50% at 0.41. Over longer horizons, the impact of recent overbought conditions fades, and uncertainty naturally increases. While the long-term trend favors a normalized (steeper) yield curve, the spread has already eclipsed late-2025 analyst expectations, creating meaningful two-way risk as Fed policy continues to evolve. Consequently, probabilities drift closer to 0.50 as the horizon extends (0.44 for 1-month out, 0.48 for 3-months out), reflecting the dispersion of outcomes and increased baseline uncertainty.
